title: Agent Evaluation Case
description: A single eval instance: an input task plus the trap and grading logic used to score an AI agent. The case is the atomic unit; families generate sweeps of cases along one construction axis.

id.description: Stable, immutable identifier. Never reused or renumbered.

version.description: Increments on any edit to this case. Identity stays fixed; version tracks change.

family.description: Reference to the parametrized generator this case belongs to. Cases in one family differ only by construction_axis / axis_level.

status.description: Lifecycle state. Retired cases are kept for provenance but excluded from runs.

suite_type.description: Governance role. regression is frozen and re-run per agent version; probe is mutable and hunts new failure modes; held_out is hidden during agent development to prevent overfitting.

capability.description: Measurement axis: the quality this case is designed to score.

construction_axis.description: Construction axis: the property of the task being varied to build the sweep.

axis_level.description: Ordinal position on the sweep, from easiest to hardest. The point where critical_check starts failing is the signal.

tags.description: Controlled-vocabulary labels. Tag names and values are English.

instruction.description: The task as presented to the agent (first user turn, or the whole prompt for single-turn cases).

artifacts.description: Input materials supplied to the agent (documents, transcripts, files).

environment.description: Tooling and side-effect surface available to the agent for this case.

tools.description: Capabilities the agent may use. 'none' means text-in/text-out only and must be the sole entry.

side_effects.description: Reversibility of agent actions. Drives the safety class of the case.

constraints.description: Explicit rules the agent must obey (scope rules, naming conventions, prohibited inferences).

expected_failure_mode.description: The specific way a weak agent is expected to fail. The core of the case; grading is built around this.

distractor.description: The element that pressures the agent toward the failure mode (pattern pressure, a tempting shortcut, an embedded instruction).

grader.description: How the run is scored. critical_check is the binary guard tied to expected_failure_mode; rubric measures graded quality.

gold.description: Path or inline reference to the reference answer, when one exists.

checker.description: Named deterministic checker, used with type 'programmatic' (e.g. findings_match).

expected_findings.description: Ground-truth planted defects for the findings_match checker.

must_not_flag.description: Compliant anchors the agent must not flag (findings_match).

rubric.description: Weighted graded criteria for fuzzy aspects. Weights should sum to 1.0.

critical_check.description: ONE binary, must-pass assertion bound to expected_failure_mode. If it fails, the case fails regardless of rubric score.

scoring.description: Aggregation rule combining critical_check and rubric into a final score.

turns.description: Multi-turn interaction script. Required for requirements_volatility cases, which inject a new constraint mid-task.

source.description: Where the input material originates (hand-authored, derived from a named document, etc.).

path.description: Location of the artifact when stored outside the case file.

content.description: Inline content when the artifact is small enough to embed.

note.description: Author note, e.g. flagging where the trap is planted.

expected_finding.$comment: severity is optional: pydantic ExpectedFinding defaults it to 'critical', so the contract must not require it.

role.description: user = normal input; inject = a new constraint introduced after work has begun; assistant = scripted prior agent turn.

allOf.$comment: Rubric-style graders must carry a rubric.
Exact-match graders must carry a gold reference.
Volatility cases must script an injected constraint mid-task.
